meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: SaaS Development for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Bridge the knowledge gap with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-friendly, expert-led guide to shipping SaaS products faster using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates SaaS complexity by providing a pre-configured architecture. By pairing this scaffold with Claude Code, you bypass manual boilerplate setup, allowing you to focus on business logic. This workflow reduces development time by 70%, enabling you to deliver professional-grade SaaS products to clients rapidly.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the cursor blinking rhythmically, mocking your lack of a formal SaaS background. Every client deadline feels like a countdown to an inevitable identity crisis. You have the vision, but the technical overhead of auth, database schema, and API integration feels like a wall you aren't equipped to climb.

But the wall is an illusion. The industry's top 1% don't build from scratch; they leverage high-leverage frameworks like Antigravity. By abstracting the boilerplate and automating the implementation via Claude Code, you transform from a struggling freelancer into a high-velocity product architect. It is time to stop coding and start shipping.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Clone the core template to establish a robust, production-ready directory structure that handles environment variables and basic routing automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity SaaS template with authentication and PostgreSQL integration.

steps.title: Define Your Business Domain
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your specific SaaS requirements onto the template architecture without touching manual configuration files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements: I need a subscription-based task manager. Update the schema and controllers accordingly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the database schema for a subscription-based task manager using Prisma.'

steps.title: Inject Business Logic
steps.description: Focus strictly on the unique value proposition of your client's app while the template handles the heavy lifting of user sessions and middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for processing task priority based on user subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a service/taskService.ts file that handles priority logic based on user roles.'

steps.title: Automate Security Middleware
steps.description: Ensure your application is hardened by leveraging the Antigravity security defaults, avoiding common vulnerabilities in early-stage SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current auth middleware and ensure all routes are protected against unauthorized access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run a security audit on all API routes and enforce JWT verification for non-public endpoints.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Prepare the build for deployment, ensuring all environment variables are correctly mapped and optimized for performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Optimize the project for deployment on Vercel, including production build scripts and environment variable validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Vercel deployment settings and optimize the production build process.'

pro_tips: Always use the '--dry-run' flag with Claude Code when modifying core schema files to prevent unintended database migrations.
Keep your 'antigravity.config.json' synced with your Git branch to ensure team consistency during client hand-offs.
Leverage the Antigravity 'mock-data' generator during the prototype phase to show clients a 'live' app before the database is fully populated.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your controllers immediately after generation to ensure your business logic never breaks during refactors.
Treat your project template as a product; document your custom tweaks so you can reuse them in future client projects for maximum margin.
content.next_steps: Once the boilerplate is deployed, focus on building one high-value feature per week to keep the client engaged and demonstrate consistent progress.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your freelance career. Build faster, charge higher rates, and deliver professional SaaS products that leave your competition behind. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
